title: Superconductivity mediated by nematic fluctuations -- the dispersion of collective modes
abstract: We analyze the spectrum of collective modes in a superconductor in which pairing is mediated by long-range nematic fluctuations. Previous experimental and theoretical studies have found that the superconducting gap in such a system is highly anisotropic and, at any finite $T<T_c$, vanishes on four arcs of the Fermi surface, even when the pairing symmetry is $s$ wave ($s^{+-}$ between hole and electron pockets). We derive the expression for the pair susceptibility $χ(\mathbf{q},Ω)$ at finite momentum $\mathbf{q}$ and frequency $Ω$ deep in the superconducting phase. We analyze the spectral function, $\operatorname{Im}χ(\mathbf{q},Ω)$, and its pole structure in the transverse (phase) and longitudinal (amplitude) channels, and compare the results with those of a conventional $s$-wave superconductor. We find that the analytic structure of the pair susceptibility in both channels is qualitatively distinct from that in a BCS superconductor. This gives rise to a highly unconventional dispersion of phase and amplitude collective modes.
summary: This paper explores how nematic fluctuations change the nature of collective modes in superconductors. It demonstrates that the resulting highly anisotropic gap leads to a unique dispersion of phase and amplitude modes that differs significantly from standard BCS theory.
main_problem: The paper investigates the dispersion and spectral structure of collective modes (transverse phase and longitudinal amplitude modes) in a superconductor where pairing is mediated by long-range nematic fluctuations.
main_result: The authors find that the analytic structure of the pair susceptibility is qualitatively different from BCS theory, exhibiting highly unconventional dispersion, including split peaks and logarithmic singularities in the longitudinal channel.
method: The study uses Gorkov's diagrammatic technique and the Random Phase Approximation (RPA) to calculate the pair-pair susceptibility and its spectral function.
model_or_system: A 2D one-band model of electrons with an anisotropic s-wave gap, specifically motivated by nematic-fluctuation-mediated superconductors like FeSe and its doped derivatives.
key_observables: Transverse (phase) susceptibility, longitudinal (amplitude) susceptibility, and their respective real and imaginary spectral functions.
important_parameters: Fermi velocity (vF), superconducting gap amplitude (delta_0), momentum transfer (q), frequency (Omega), and coupling strength (g).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes a zero-temperature limit, a 2D electron system, and neglects long-range Coulomb interactions in certain parts of the derivation.
figures_summary: The notes mention a polar plot (Fig 1) and a frequency dependence plot (Fig 9).
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of nematic-mediated pairing, defines the Hamiltonian and anisotropic gap model, derives the pair susceptibility using diagrammatic techniques, analyzes the pole structure and dispersion in both transverse and longitudinal channels, and provides mathematical proofs for the analytic properties of the susceptibility in an appendix.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
